author: @calculatedrush
text: You can do more with this! Create a reaction vessel and you now have an arc furnace capable of making things like carbides such as calcium carbide or tungsten carbide. Simply by reacting carbon and calcium together in an arc furnace without oxygen will produce calcium carbide. This allows access to acetylene.

author: @raydan18
text: as soon as you open the MW cover just short the capacitor terminals with the insulating pliers to discharge it and one hand in the pocket.  Those caps have the discharge resistor inside to drop the voltage so it's safe to handle but this resistor can fail and better to get in to habbit to always discharge them by shorting

text: mini arc furnace

author: @ThatTweedle
text: IDK if this is a good idea to have as a YT "Shorts" because you didn't explain any of the safety or dangers of it... plus you didn't say "Don't Try This At Home!" -  As much as I believe in the free spread of information and knowledge we just don't live in a society of personal responsibility....  if some kid sees this then takes apart his mom's microwave and shocks himself unconscious (or WORSE) the mom is gonna IMMEDIATELY BLAME YOU - THROUGH HER LAWYER!!! And YOU won't stand a chance - well.... not on America... which is exactly where that dumbass kid will live....  so you might want to re-think this one :(

replies.author: @ThatTweedle
replies.text:
I'm sorry, but I'm just trying to look out for you... on the internet you go to JAIL for making a MEME saying you can VOTE by phone by sending a TEXT - 7MONTHS IN JAIL FOR A MEME!!!! - So you REALLY should just put a line of text on the top of the entire video, that stays there there entire time, that says: "DISCLAIMER: DO NOT TRY THIS AT HOME! VERY DANGEROUS!"
Then you have NOTHING to worry about :) Better SAFE than SORRY. :)

author: @toddburgess6792
text: I've been warned of high powered capacitors in microwave ovens whacking people into the next realm of existence. That's my only worry about taking one apart, I have no clue what's what.

replies.author: @peterkemp8633
replies.text: The microwave hv, capacitor has a big resistor in it which prevents a deadly charge remaining in there too long, as long as you know what you are doing you can remain safe,

replies.author: @ArbitraryOnslaught
replies.text: I read on a forum of someone who was acustom to taking them apart and had been shocked and survived. Its not pretty. Best to avoid this if you are not prepared. I did as much safety as possible thick rubber gloves chicken stick, seperate switched circuit ect. And when an allen key welded to the stick I almost touched it to knock it off 😂 im no measure of brillieantz thu. 😅
